academic_sources.title: SEND in England
academic_sources.author: DfE
academic_sources.key_finding: GRT SEND rate 35%, Roma 32%, Pakistani/Bangladeshi 18.2%, national avg 14.9%

academic_sources.title: Births by parents' country of birth
academic_sources.author: ONS
academic_sources.key_finding: Pakistani/Bangladeshi TFR ~2.3 vs White British 1.55 — converging but still 48% higher

academic_sources.title: Permanent Exclusions and Suspensions
academic_sources.author: DfE
academic_sources.key_finding: GRT permanent exclusion rate 2.8× national average

academic_sources.title: The Casey Review: Opportunity and Integration
academic_sources.author: Dame Louise Casey
academic_sources.key_finding: Social segregation, school governance issues in areas with high Muslim populations; Burnley, Blackburn, Bradford highlighted

academic_sources.title: Community Cohesion: A Report of the Independent Review Team
academic_sources.author: Ted Cantle
academic_sources.key_finding: Northern towns living 'parallel lives' — Bradford, Oldham, Burnley. Lack of inter-community contact

academic_sources.title: Integrated Communities Strategy Green Paper
academic_sources.author: MHCLG
academic_sources.key_finding: 5 Integration Areas designated including Blackburn with Darwen, Burnley, Pendle

academic_sources.title: Electoral integrity in areas with diverse communities
academic_sources.author: Electoral Commission
academic_sources.key_finding: Targeted reports on electoral fraud risks — Tower Hamlets, Bradford, Birmingham

academic_sources.title: Ethnic group inequalities in housing and neighbourhood quality
academic_sources.author: Jivraj & Simpson, University of Manchester
academic_sources.key_finding: Selective outmigration of higher-income White British from areas of rapid demographic change

academic_sources.title: The Fiscal Impact of Immigration
academic_sources.author: Migration Observatory, University of Oxford
academic_sources.key_finding: Recent migrants contribute less in taxes initially; fiscal impact depends on age, skills, family size

academic_sources.title: LGR Cost Analysis Reports
academic_sources.author: National Audit Office
academic_sources.key_finding: Buckinghamshire 25% overrun, Dorset 4-month delay, North Yorkshire IT issues, Northamptonshire emergency

academic_sources.title: Council Tax: The Case for Reform
academic_sources.author: Institute for Fiscal Studies
academic_sources.key_finding: Collection rate disparities linked to deprivation — deprived areas 2-4% lower collection

academic_sources.title: The Ties That Bind
academic_sources.author: Demos
academic_sources.key_finding: Integration challenges in northern towns with high South Asian populations; parallel institutions

academic_sources.title: Immigration Statistics: Asylum and Resettlement
academic_sources.author: Home Office
academic_sources.key_finding: Quarterly asylum dispersal by local authority; accommodation types; trend data

precedents.outcome: Significant IT integration issues persisting 18 months post-vesting
Emergency government intervention; commissioners appointed pre-vesting

risk_factors: 22 months — shortest UK LGR timeline ever attempted (precedent avg: 32 months)
15 councils — most councils ever merged in single LGR (precedent avg: 8)
1.6M population — largest LGR area ever (precedent avg: 572K)
~1,200 IT systems to integrate (24+ months typical migration timeline)
30,000+ staff requiring TUPE (6-month legal minimum consultation)
IT migration alone requires 24 months — exceeds 22-month total timeline
Service continuity gap probability estimated at 73% based on precedent data

bradford_oldham_comparison.trajectory_narrative: East Lancashire's demographic, fiscal, and deprivation profile mirrors Bradford circa 2010. Bradford: 30.5% Muslim, IMD 34.2, collection rate 93.2%, DSG deficit £64M, s114 warning 2024. Oldham: 24.3% Muslim, IMD 32.8, collection rate 94.1%, children's services 'Requires Improvement'. An East Lancashire unitary (Pennine Lancashire in 3/4-unitary models) would concentrate similar demographics with even weaker fiscal foundations — fewer resources, higher dependency, declining tax base. Without sustained investment, the Bradford/Oldham fiscal decline trajectory is the most likely outcome within 10-15 years.
